meta.name: r
meta.title: R Skill
meta.description: R language and document formats (R, Rmd, Quarto): coding standards and Copilot guidance for idiomatic, safe, and consistent code generation. Triggers on: **/*.R, **/*.r, **/*.Rmd, **/*.rmd, **/*.qmd

original_definition.skill_description: R language and document formats (R, Rmd, Quarto): coding standards and Copilot guidance for idiomatic, safe, and consistent code generation. Triggers on: **/*.R, **/*.r, **/*.Rmd, **/*.rmd, **/*.qmd

actions.name: Help GitHub Copilot generate idiomatic, safe, and maintainab
actions.description: Help GitHub Copilot generate idiomatic, safe, and maintainable R code across projects.

actions.name: Magrittr pipe `%>%`: Continue using in projects already comm
actions.description: Magrittr pipe `%>%`: Continue using in projects already committed to magrittr or when you need features like `.`, `%T>%`, or `%$%`.

actions.name: Large datasets: consider `data.table`; benchmark with your w
actions.description: Large datasets: consider `data.table`; benchmark with your workload.

actions.name: dplyr compatibility: Use `dtplyr` to write dplyr syntax that
actions.description: dplyr compatibility: Use `dtplyr` to write dplyr syntax that translates to data.table operations automatically for performance gains.

actions.name: Pre-commit: consider `precommit` hooks to lint/format automa
actions.description: Pre-commit: consider `precommit` hooks to lint/format automatically.

actions.name: Tests: prefer small, pure, composable functions that are eas
actions.description: Tests: prefer small, pure, composable functions that are easy to unit test.

actions.name: Dependencies: manage with `renv`; snapshot after adding pack
actions.description: Dependencies: manage with `renv`; snapshot after adding packages.

actions.name: Strings & Dates: use `stringr`/`lubridate` where already pre
actions.description: Strings & Dates: use `stringr`/`lubridate` where already present; otherwise use clear base helpers (e.g., `nchar()`, `substr()`, `as.Date()` with expl

actions.name: I/O: prefer explicit, typed readers (e.g., `readr::read_csv(
actions.description: I/O: prefer explicit, typed readers (e.g., `readr::read_csv()`); make parsing assumptions explicit.

actions.name: Prefer `ggplot2` for publication-quality plots. Keep layers
actions.description: Prefer `ggplot2` for publication-quality plots. Keep layers readable and label axes and units.

actions.name: In tidyverse contexts, use `rlang::abort()` / `rlang::warn()
actions.description: In tidyverse contexts, use `rlang::abort()` / `rlang::warn()` for structured conditions; in base-only code, use `stop()` / `warning()`.

actions.name: When multiple approaches are equivalent, prefer readability
actions.description: When multiple approaches are equivalent, prefer readability and type stability and explain the trade-offs.

rules.description: Be consistent: Don't mix `|>` and `%>%` within the same script unless there's a clear technical reason.
Prefer consistent return structures—typed outputs for normal flows, structured lists only when error details are required.
Validate inputs with `req()` and clear, user-friendly messages.
Use connection pooling (`pool`) for databases; avoid long-lived global objects.
Avoid global state; prefer local helpers. Use `withr::with_seed()` for deterministic chunks.

directives.description: Match the project’s style. If the file shows a preference (tidyverse vs. base R, `%>%` vs. `|>`), follow it.
Prefer clear, vectorized code. Keep functions small and avoid hidden side effects.
Qualify non-base functions in examples/snippets, e.g., `dplyr::mutate()`, `stringr::str_detect()`. In project code, using `library()` is acceptable wh
Naming: `lower_snake_case` for objects/files; avoid dots in names.
Side effects: Never call `setwd()`; prefer project-relative paths (e.g., `here::here()`).
Reproducibility: Set seeds locally around stochastic operations using `withr::with_seed()`.
Validation: Validate and constrain user inputs; use typed checks and allowlists where possible.
Safety: Avoid `eval(parse())`, unvalidated shell calls, and unparameterized SQL.
Native pipe `|>` (R ≥ 4.1.0): Prefer in R ≥ 4.1 (no extra dependency).
Profiling: Use `profvis::profvis()` to identify performance bottlenecks in your code. Profile before optimizing.
